title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, Raiders minority owner, reportedly influenced trade decisions involving Maxx Crosby, preventing a Patriots acquisition.
Maxx Crosby was ultimately traded to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall pick in 2026.
The Patriots may seek edge rushers in free agency, with Jaelan Phillips and Boye Mafe as potential targets.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Alec Pierce is expected to command over $27 million annually as he potentially enters free agency.
The Colts are negotiating with Pierce to re-sign him before the legal tampering period begins.
Pierce led the NFL in yards per reception the last two seasons, recording 1,003 yards and six touchdowns in 2025.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Boston Public Library.
Flynn overcame dyslexia and ADHD with support from libraries, which she views as vital accessible resources.
Despite a rare vascular condition and four leg surgeries, Flynn is determined to complete the marathon.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient, Christina, facing advanced breast cancer.
Christina, an experienced marathon runner, inspires Comander with her resilience and belief that 'exercise is medicine' during treatment.
The marathon supports the Ellie Fund, aiding breast cancer patients with services like transportation, meals, and childcare reimbursement.

title: Here’s why a trade for Brian Thomas Jr. would be a home run move for the Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Brian Thomas Jr. is considered a potential trade target for the Patriots, offering high upside despite a down 2025 season.
Thomas recorded 1,300 scrimmage yards, 10 touchdowns in 2024, and is under contract for $4 million in 2026.
Acquiring Thomas would allow the Patriots financial flexibility to strengthen other positions while maintaining roster stability.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Ryan Gosling hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on March 7 for his fourth time, alongside musical guest Gorillaz.
Gosling's episode included memorable sketches such as 'Otezla' and 'Passing Notes' with Ashley Padilla.
Gorillaz performed their first single 'Clint Eastwood' to celebrate their 25th anniversary at Studio 8H.

title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, from New Bedford, was identified as the deceased crew member from the capsized Yankee Rose.
The search for the second missing crew member was called off due to adverse weather conditions.
The Yankee Rose, found capsized near Provincetown, had a previous incident on February 26 involving carbon monoxide exposure.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are monitoring Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs as he approaches free agency, per ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.
Doubs recorded 724 receiving yards and six touchdowns for the Packers last season, leading his team in receptions.
New England is also linked to Colts' Alec Pierce and considers trading for Eagles' A.J. Brown this offseason.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The man whose skull was found in Bristol, N.H. in 1986 has been identified as Warren Kuchinsky.
Kuchinsky, last known alive in the mid-1970s, was identified using forensic genetic genealogy and a DNA match.
The DNA Doe Project and New Hampshire authorities found no evidence of foul play in Kuchinsky's death.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn of scam involving fake parking violation notices appearing to be from Boston Municipal Court.
Fraudulent notices feature Massachusetts state seal and a QR code for payment, which leads to the scam.
Residents are urged to verify parking tickets through the city's website and report scams to police.
